title: How to find duplicate values in a column
task: Flag every value in a list that appears more than once.

excel.note: Put it next to the first value and fill down.

explanation: COUNTIF counts how many times the current row's value appears in the whole list. A count above 1 means the value is duplicated somewhere, so the IF labels it "Duplicate". The $ signs lock the list range so the formula fills down correctly. In the sample list, "apple" appears twice, so its row is flagged.
